mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in retail sales signals robust consumer spending and aggregate demand, which drives upward revisions to corporate revenue forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in RSAFS coincide with negative SPX 20d forward returns in more than 55% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5% signals restrictive monetary policy that elevates discount rates and borrowing costs for nonfinancial corporations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days as leveraged cash flows are repriced lower.
failure_condition: Instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% do not produce significantly negative mean SPX 60d returns compared to a neutral policy baseline over a 3-year rolling window (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in industrial production and payroll employment confirm a broad-based expansion in real output and labor income, reducing recession risk and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days that exceed either single-series signal.
failure_condition: The joint positive movement of INDPRO and PAYEMS fails to predict positive SPX 20d returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a tight labor market, positive month-over-month changes in housing starts reflect durable household formation demand and construction sector momentum that supports corporate earnings in cyclical industries, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 4.5%, month-over-month increases in HOUST coincide with negative SPX 60d returns in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month contraction in industrial production indicates weakening manufacturing output and falling capacity utilization, which drives immediate negative earnings revisions and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month declines in INDPRO coincide with positive SPX 5d returns in more than 55% of observations over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 5.0% signals slack in the labor market that reduces wage income growth and consumer spending power, triggering negative wealth effects and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: Periods with UNRATE above 5.0% do not exhibit significantly negative mean SPX 60d returns compared to the full-sample baseline over a 3-year rolling window (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A high federal funds rate combined with a month-over-month increase in the consumer price index produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days through joint discount rate and cost pressures.
failure_condition: A regression of SPX 20d returns on the interaction of CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS yields a coefficient with the wrong sign or a p-value above 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above its trailing four-quarter average signals accelerating aggregate demand and reinforces recession-denial pricing, producing positive S&P 500 returns in the five trading days following the advance release.
failure_condition: Quarter-over-quarter annualized GDPC1 growth in the top tercile fails to coincide with positive SPX 5d returns following the release in more than 50% of cases over a 5-year window.
